    {
      "title": "Multiple Assessment Activities",
      "source_text": "Quiz, debate, role play, viva-voce, group discussion, visual expression, interactive bulletin boards, gallery walks, exit cards, concept maps, peer assessment, self-assessment etc.",

    },

      "title": "Art Integration Activities",

      "related_chapter": ""
    }
  ],
  "projects": [
    {
      "title": "Class IX Disaster Management Project",
      "source_text": "Every student must undertake one project on Disaster Management. Objectives: Create awareness, prepare to face situations, ensure participation in risk reduction plans.",
      "related_unit": "Internal Assessment (IX)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Class IX Interdisciplinary Project",
      "source_text": "History Chapter IV Forest Society and Colonialism & Geography Chapter 5 Natural Vegetation and Wildlife. Compare forest situations, evaluate commercial forestry, analyse rebellions.",
      "related_unit": "Livelihoods (IX History) / Environment (IX Geography)",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter IV & V"
    },
    {
      "title": "Class X Project Work",
      "source_text": "Consumer Awareness OR Social Issues OR Sustainable Development. Objectives: Gain insight, apply concepts, enhance life skills.",
      "related_unit": "Internal Assessment (X)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Class X Interdisciplinary Project",
      "source_text": "History Chapter III Making of a Global World & Geography Chapter 7 Lifelines of National Economy & Economics Chapter 4 Globalisation. Analyse implications, discuss globalisation experiences.",
      "related_unit": "Livelihoods (X History) / Economic Activities (X Geography)",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter III, VII, IV"
    }
  ],
  "assessment_information": {
    "marks_distribution": [
      "Class IX: Total Marks - 80 (Theory) + 20 (Internal Assessment). Theory includes Map Pointing.",
      "Class X: Total Marks - 80 (Board Exam) + 20 (Internal Assessment).",
      "Question Paper Design Class X: MCQs (1 Mark, 25%), Long Answer Questions (2-3 Marks), Case Study Questions (4 Marks), Map Pointing.",
      "Competency Weightage Class X: Remembering/Understanding (30%), Applying (13.25%), Analysing/Evaluating/Creating (50%)."
    ],
    "excluded_or_non_assessed_content": [
      "Pastoralists in the Modern World (IX History) - assessed only as part of Periodic Assessment",
      "The Story of Village Palampur (IX Economics) - assessed only as part of Periodic Assessment",
      "Natural Vegetation and Wildlife Map Pointing (IX Geography) - Only map pointing evaluated annually, content not in annual exam marks distribution explicitly separate from other chapters but noted for specific evaluation.",
      "The Age of Industrialisation (X History) - assessed only as part of Periodic Assessment",
      "Lifelines of National Economy Map Pointing (X Geography) - Only map pointing evaluated in Board Examination"
    ],
    "teacher_notes": [
      "Interdisciplinary projects must be carried out at school between April and September. Carryover to home is strictly avoided.",
      "Projects should use eco-friendly products without excessive expenditure.",
      "Project records (internal assessment) must be maintained for three months after result declaration.",
      "Use of Govt. of India website maps is permitted.",
      "Rubrics for project evaluation: Content accuracy/originality (2), Competencies/Presentation (2), Viva-Voce (1).",
      "Map work includes locating, labeling, and identifying specific areas as listed in the syllabus sections."
    ]
  }
}
